name: error-sanitization-process
purpose: All error-producing code passes error messages through sanitize-error.cjs before logging or returning, ensuring home paths, tokens, and connection strings never leak into logs or user output.
notes: 61+ unique callers confirmed by D7 (46 scripts/ + 15 .claude/). sanitize-error.js ESM twin exists in SoNash but not JASON-OS. JASON-OS calls .cjs 'canonical' while SoNash calls it 'CJS wrapper for .js ESM twin'. The process CONTRACT is: every caller must require/import sanitize-error before any error path. No automated enforcement — purely behavioral. D7 confirms safe-fs.js, security-helpers.js, and sanitize-error.cjs together form the three-layer error-safety stack. 3 callers in .claude/hooks/ use inline fallback patterns if lib is absent (degrades gracefully).

name: state-file-lifecycle-process
purpose: Hooks write session-scoped state to .claude/state/ and .claude/hooks/ hidden files; skills and scripts read that state to restore context across compaction, session restart, and user-invoked recovery.
notes: Two-tier state architecture: .claude/hooks/.* (ephemeral, per-session, hidden dot-files) vs .claude/state/* (persistent, survives compaction). D17b documents this in STATE_SCHEMA.md which self-annotates as covering only 15 of 113 actual state files. D3a confirms 9 hidden state files in .claude/hooks/ root alone. Producers and consumers are distinct hook events — pre-compaction-save (PreCompact) produces handoff.json, compact-restore (SessionStart) consumes it. session-start.js writes .session-state.json; track-agent-invocation.js writes .session-agents.json. 15+ distinct state file paths catalogued across D3a+D3b findings.

name: hook-warning-escalation-process
purpose: Hook events append warnings to a shared store; user acknowledges per-type; sync-warnings-ack bumps the lastCleared pointer; pre-push escalation gate blocks pushes if any unacknowledged error-severity warnings remain.
notes: D6a confirms 10 unique caller files, ~25+ call-sites for append-hook-warning.js. D6d identifies this as a 3-script trio: append (write) + resolve (mark resolved) + sync-warnings-ack (flush/advance lastCleared pointer). D6d confirms sync-warnings-ack.js is NOT in JASON-OS — HIGH priority port gap. Pre-push escalation gate is in .husky/pre-push (git layer) AND block-push-to-main.js (Claude Code layer) for two-layer enforcement. statusline D5 widget reads hook-warnings-ack.json to show unacked warning count. D6a confirms severity escalation: info->warning at 5+ occurrences in 7d, warning->error at 15+ occurrences. append-hook-warning.js IS in JASON-OS; sync-warnings-ack.js is NOT. Port completeness: 1/3 scripts ported.

name: canonical-memory-promotion-process
purpose: User-home memory files accumulate session learnings; canonical-memory/ holds the last committed snapshot; the gap between them (60 of 82 files missing canonical counterparts in SoNash) represents unpromoted learnings requiring manual diff, review, and canonical commit.
notes: D5 confirms: 25 canonical files, 83 user-home files, 23 shared (all differing), 60 gap files (73.2% of user-home have no canonical counterpart). All 23 shared files differ — canonical is never fully in sync. Promotion is manual: no automation exists. D5 identifies 3 categories of drift: formatting-only (13 files), substantive content additions (7 files), operationally wrong staleness (3 files — feedback_stale_reviews_dist references removed command, user_expertise_profile wrong framing, project_agent_env_analysis shows completed work as in-progress). Two canonical-only files are orphaned (feedback_parallel_agents_for_impl absorbed, feedback_verify_not_grep renamed). The process gap: no tooling enforces canonical promotion; it happens ad hoc when operators manually diff and commit.

name: skill-registry-update-process
purpose: When a new skill or command is added, generate-skill-registry.js scans .claude/skills/ and rebuilds the llms.txt registry; generate-documentation-index.mjs rebuilds DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md; both must run to keep LLM discovery and human navigation in sync.
notes: D6d confirms generate-skill-registry.js (164L, CJS, sanitize-then-portable) and generate-documentation-index.mjs (1147L, native ESM .mjs, not-portable). generate-documentation-index.mjs is the LARGEST script in all scripts/ root at 1147 lines. It is the only .mjs file in scripts/ — cannot be require()d from CJS. D6d also confirms validate-skill-config.js (341L, CJS, sanitize-then-portable) validates skill configs against scripts/config/skill-config.json. The 3-script chain: add skill -> validate skill config -> regenerate registry -> regenerate doc index. The .mjs file's not-portable rating is due to hardcoded SoNash dir structure. JASON-OS has its own llms.txt (D17a confirmed) but the regeneration script is not confirmed ported. Port risk: regeneration scripts may diverge silently if not co-ported.

name: mcp-server-registration-process
purpose: MCP servers are declared in .mcp.json (explicit) or auto-discovered by Claude Code plugins; settings.json controls permissions for mcp__ tool calls; together they gate which external services Claude can invoke during a session.
sanitize_fields: API keys (must use env var substitution)
project-specific MCP tool names in settings.json
notes: D17b confirms three .mcp.json variants in SoNash: active .mcp.json (2 explicit entries — memory + sonarcloud), .mcp.json.bak (uses ${SONAR_TOKEN} env-var pattern), .mcp.json.example (9 servers with inline docs, empty-string placeholders). Active file is minimal because Claude Code auto-discovers Firebase, GitHub, Context7, Playwright, Episodic Memory via plugin system — these do NOT need explicit .mcp.json entries. The settings.json controls MCP tool permissions via allow/deny lists. D17b also confirms .vscode/mcp.json uses promptString input for CONTEXT7_API_KEY — the VS Code-native secret pattern. JASON-OS gap: no .gemini/ config equivalent. The process: add server to .mcp.json -> restart session -> new mcp__<server>__<tool> tools appear -> add to settings.json allow list if needed. No automation — purely manual registration.

name: agent-spawn-pipeline-process
purpose: Skills dispatch named agents via Task tool calls with context_skills injection; agents execute with constrained tool sets and maxTurns; results return to the orchestrating skill for synthesis or further dispatch.
sanitize_fields: agent tool allowlists for SoNash-specific MCP tools
notes: D20a confirms 430 edges in the skill/agent dependency graph, 76 'spawns' edges. context injection pattern: all 32 project-scoped agents in SoNash have 'skills: [sonash-context]' in frontmatter — this is the mechanism for injecting SoNash-specific context into every agent. JASON-OS agents lack this injection (D2a confirms). D2a identifies 7 deep-research pipeline agents, all with specific pipeline_phase assignments. The spawn contract: skill calls Task({agent: name, context_skills: [...], ...}); agent is loaded with tools from frontmatter; agent executes; result text returned to spawner. D3b confirms track-agent-invocation.js PostToolUse hook tracks all agent invocations to .session-agents.json for compliance checks. Windows 0-byte bug (T23) means agent output_file is unreliable on Windows — D21a deep-research composite documents the persistence safety net workaround (Phase 2.5). Model assignment: 14 sonnet, 2 opus (heavyweight architectural agents), 1 inherit (debugger). Orphan agents: 18 identified by D20a — all intentional leaf skills or SoNash-product-specific tools.

name: build-and-install-tools-process
purpose: Go source in tools/statusline/ is compiled by build.sh, the binary is versioned and installed to ~/.claude/statusline/, and settings.json statusLine.command points to the installed binary; cache files accumulate at runtime in ~/.claude/statusline/cache/.
notes: D13 confirms 22 widgets SoNash, 16 widgets JASON-OS (6 dropped: A6 active agent, B3 worktree, D1 hook health, D5 unacked warnings, F15 uptime, I4 session count). D13 confirms source_scope=universal, runtime_scope=machine for cache.go — canonical example of source/runtime split. Build pipeline: go test -> go build [-a] -> copy binary+config to ~/.claude/statusline/. Versioning convention: <project-slug>-statusline-vN — enables safe in-place replacement (bump N, never overwrite running exe). Security finding: config.local.toml is gitignored and not committed despite initial agent error report. Cache namespace collision: both SoNash and JASON-OS binaries write to same ~/.claude/statusline/cache/ dir on shared machine — github-pr.json and github-ci.json are project-agnostic filenames that get overwritten. Port completeness: 16/22 widgets. D13 priority order for back-port: D1+D5 (hook health+warnings) -> A6 (agent) -> I4 (session count) -> B3 (worktree) -> F15 (uptime).

name: debt-escalation-process
purpose: Technical debt items are ingested from multiple sources (audits, SonarCloud, PR-deferred, manual), normalized, deduplicated across 6 passes, assigned DEBT-XXXX IDs, stored in MASTER_DEBT.jsonl, escalated by severity, and resolved through the debt-runner skill workflow.
notes: D9 confirms 28 files in scripts/debt/, 6-layer pipeline (extraction/intake -> normalization -> dedup -> triage -> master storage/views -> resolution/lifecycle). MASTER_DEBT.jsonl has 4500+ items in SoNash. Dedup pipeline has 6 passes including semantic (Levenshtein >90%) and cross-source (SonarCloud rule <-> audit finding correlation). JASON-OS has add-debt v0.1 stub (appends to .planning/DEBT_LOG.md) — not TDMS-capable. D21a confirms 3 composite workflows are TDMS-blocked (audit-family, ecosystem-audit-family, debt-runner). Port path: all 28 scripts/debt/*.js must port together. generate-content-hash.js and normalize-file-path.js (D7) are also TDMS-specific lib deps. D20a confirms add-debt has 15 inbound edges — it is the universal TDMS intake sink for every skill with deferred findings. Highest-complexity non-portable process in SoNash.

fields: intermediate normalized/deduped JSONL before master promotion

name: cross-model-gemini-check-process
purpose: After Phase 3 adversarial challenge in deep-research, findings are cross-checked against Gemini CLI output; if Gemini diverges materially, dispute-resolver applies DRAGged conflict resolution and the final synthesizer re-synthesizes before presenting findings.
notes: D21a confirmed deep-research workflow is PORTED (v2.0 in both repos). The Gemini cross-check is documented in deep-research MEMORY feedback (feedback_deep_research_phases_mandatory.md): Phase 3 -> Gemini CLI -> re-synthesize if divergent. D2a confirms contrarian-challenger (Phase 3), otb-challenger (Phase 3), dispute-resolver (Phase 3.5) are all in .claude/agents/. The cross-model check is a behavioral process, not a data pipeline — it relies on the operator running `gemini --model gemini-2.5-pro` in the terminal (external CLI, not an MCP server). The Gemini CLI is referenced in .gemini/config.yaml (D17b). The dispute-resolver applies DRAG (Disagreement Resolution Across Grounds) protocol when Gemini and Claude materially disagree on a key claim. D17b confirms JASON-OS does not have .gemini/ configured — the Gemini cross-check is available (Gemini CLI exists) but unconfigured for project-scoped styleguide. The process is partially gated by the MANDATORY feedback memory (Phases 3-5 cannot be skipped).

consumers: Gemini CLI invocations during deep-research Phase 3

name: qodo-sonarcloud-feedback-loop-process
path: GitHub PR + Qodo + SonarCloud + pr-review skill
purpose: After a PR is created, Qodo and SonarCloud bots post review comments; the operator pastes those comments into the pr-review skill (not fetched via gh — pastes only); pr-review skill processes them per DAS framework; fixes are committed and pushed; the loop continues until all bots and reviewers are satisfied.
notes: D1d confirms pr-review PORTED to JASON-OS at v4.6-jasonos-v0.1 (CodeRabbit dropped, Gemini reviewer dropped, TDMS replaced with add-debt stub, reference set trimmed). MEMORY feedback_pr_review_paste_only: never fetch PR review content via gh; user paste is source of truth (gh truncates, doesn't expand collapsed sections). MEMORY feedback_sonarcloud_mark_as_safe: code changes alone don't clear SonarCloud hotspots — operator must manually mark as Safe/Fixed in UI. D17b confirms .qodo/ exists in SoNash dotdirs. D10a+D10b cover scripts/reviews/ TypeScript subsystem (not yet read in this scan). The sonarcloud skill (D1f) orchestrates SonarCloud operations against TDMS — not-portable as standalone, but the pr-review workflow integration is portable. D17b confirms sonarcloud + Qodo are 'preferred' per reference_pr_review_integrations.md memory. Process gate: MUST wait for Qodo review before starting fix cycle (PR timing discipline feedback).

name: session-counter-coupling-process
path: SESSION_CONTEXT.md + hooks reading session # + scripts logging session #
purpose: SESSION_CONTEXT.md carries the authoritative session counter; multiple hooks read it at event time to tag log entries with the session number; scripts like governance-logger.js and commit-tracker.js use the session counter as a correlation key across JSONL log files.
sanitize_fields: session counter format (Session #NNN vs alternate formats)
notes: D3a confirms: commit-tracker.js, check-remote-session-context.js, and governance-logger.js all read SESSION_CONTEXT.md for the session counter. D3a notes: 'SESSION_CONTEXT.md session counter convention — a project convention (not a lib helper) that downstream ports must replicate or the counter returns null (gracefully).' D6c confirms log-session-activity.js writes .claude/session-activity.jsonl with session context. D6d confirms session-end-commit.js reads SESSION_CONTEXT.md and updates 'Uncommitted Work' field, supporting BOTH the legacy SoNash inline-bold format AND the JASON-OS D12 5-field heading format. JASON-OS session-end-commit.js is actually AHEAD of SoNash on this (JASON-OS has dual-format support). The session counter is the spine that correlates log entries across: commit-log.jsonl, governance-changes.jsonl, override-log.jsonl, session-activity.jsonl, and hook-warnings-log.jsonl. No formal schema for SESSION_CONTEXT.md — the format is maintained by convention and read by regex.

name: self-audit-regeneration-process
purpose: Each major skill has a companion self-audit script that reads SKILL.md, checks for compliance with skill authoring standards (phases, guard rails, compaction resilience, invocation tracking), produces DECISIONS.md-style output, and drives skill content updates.
notes: D1a confirms hook-ecosystem-audit and script-ecosystem-audit are self-contained (no _shared/ecosystem-audit/ dep); these are the best port candidates. D1a also confirms skill-ecosystem-audit uses _shared/ecosystem-audit/ (6 lib files). The copy-not-import pattern: 8 copies of safe-fs.js embedded in skill scripts/lib/ dirs (D7). This is intentional design for self-contained deployment but creates a maintenance hazard — any fix to scripts/lib/safe-fs.js must propagate to all 8 copies. D20a confirms _shared/AUDIT_TEMPLATE.md has 9 inbound edges (consumed by all single-session audit-* skills). D1a confirms all 5 mega audit skills have 'run-{domain}-ecosystem-audit.js' entry points. The self-audit process is: (1) skill audit runs checkers, (2) findings presented interactively, (3) user decides accept/defer, (4) accepts become skill updates, (5) defers go to TDMS via /add-debt. Per MEMORY feedback_per_skill_self_audit: 'almost every skill gets its own self-audit scripted from shared base, adjusted per skill'.
